cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to find out which statement is true based on the passage provided. The question has four options: A, B, C, D. Let me read through the passage again carefully and check each option one by one. The passage starts by talking about the collapse of a big wild salmon run on the West Coast. It mentions that the Sacramento River salmon run is usually productive but last fall only about 90,000 adult salmon returned, which is the second lowest number on record. So that's option A: "Last fall, there was the second lowest number of grown salmon returning on record." The passage says "about 90,000 adult salmon returned... the second lowest number on record," so A seems correct. Let me check the other options to be sure. Option B: "The salmon migrates to the ocean when they grow up." The passage says: "wild salmon are born in streams and rivers, migrate to the ocean when they're juveniles..." So they migrate when they're juveniles, not when they grow up. So B is false. Option C: "The farm-raised salmon is not as delicious and nutrients as the wild salmon." The passage mentions that there will be abundant farm-raised salmon, but doesn't compare the taste or nutrition to wild salmon. So there's no information in the passage to support C.
